items.title: Here’s what happened in crypto today
items.summary: Need to know what happened in crypto today? Here is the latest news on daily trends and events impacting Bitcoin price, blockchain, DeFi, NFTs, Web3 and crypto regulation.

items.title: Google Nano Banana 2 vs ByteDance Seedream 5.0 Lite: Which AI Image Generator Is Best?
items.summary: A hands-on comparison between Google's Nano Banana 2 and ByteDance's Seedream 5.0 shows how the latest image models differ on price, speed, and creative control.

items.title: CoinDesk 20 performance update: AAVE plunges 10%, leading index lower
items.summary: Cardano (ADA), down 5.9% from Monday, joined Aave (AAVE) as an underperformer.

items.title: Visa and Stripe’s Bridge plan stablecoin card expansion to over 100 countries
items.summary: Visa and Stripe-owned Bridge aim to expand stablecoin-linked Visa cards to 18 countries, and plan 100-plus by the end of the year, while testing stablecoin settlement with Lead Bank.

items.title: Ondo Finance tokenized stocks platform on Binance wins regulatory approval in Abu Dhabi
items.summary: The regulatory nod will let UAE-based financial institutions deal in tokenized equities on Binance's regulated trading venue.

items.title: Strive strategist says AI deflation could push Bitcoin to $11M by 2036
items.summary: Strive’s Joe Burnett argues AI-driven deflation may force looser policy, pushing Bitcoin toward $11 million a coin by 2036 and a $230 trillion market cap.

items.title: Ether price again rejected at $2K: How low can ETH go in March?
items.summary: Ether needed to hold a key support recently established at $1,800, coinciding with the lower trend line of a classic chart pattern that warns of a drop below $1,500.

items.title: Visa and Bridge plan stablecoin-linked card expansion to over 100 countries
items.summary: Bridge has partnered with Lead Bank, a participant in Visa’s stablecoin settlement pilot, to help businesses and fintechs offer stablecoin-backed Visa cards.

items.title: Iranian Exchange Outflows Spiked to $10.3M Amid Airstrikes: Chainalysis
items.summary: Some $10.3 million left Iranian exchanges in the days after joint US-Israeli strikes, with hourly volumes approaching $2 million at the peak.

items.title: Aave Chan Initiative to exit Aave DAO after governance clash over funding
items.summary: The move follows a 52.58% Snapshot vote on a $42.5 million funding package for Aave Labs, now headed to the ARFC stage before any binding onchain decision.

items.title: Morning Minute: The Banks Are Coming for Crypto Custody
items.summary: It's no longer a matter of if major banks will custody crypto but a matter of when, with Morgan Stanley, Citigroup and Barclays all lining up.

items.title: Bitcoin could slide further on liquidity squeeze, but long-term bull case intact: Sygnum CIO
items.summary: Fabian Dori says a short-term liquidity squeeze is driving crypto’s slump, with further downside possible, though improving macro data and fundamentals could speed a recovery.

items.title: Deloitte signs off on Anchorage reserve report for Tether’s USAT stablecoin
items.summary: Big Four firm Deloitte attested to $17.6 million in reserves backing USAt, Tether’s new US-regulated stablecoin issued by Anchorage Digital Bank.

items.title: Bybit claims new fraud system stopped $300M of risky withdrawals in Q4 2025
items.summary: Bybit says most of the $300 million “saved” came from users backing out after real-time warnings, highlighting how exchanges are shifting from recovery to preemptive fraud stops.

items.title: Oil shock and inflation fears drag down bitcoin
items.summary: Your day-ahead look for March 3, 2026

items.title: Japan PM Takaichi disavows ‘Sanae Token’ after memecoin hits $28M peak
items.summary: Japanese PM Sanae Takaichi said she had no knowledge of the token, as Kyodo reported the FSA was considering whether unregistered operators were involved.

items.title: Four Headwinds Stalling Bitcoin's $70K Breakout
items.summary: Bitcoin pulled back from Monday’s peak near $70,000 as geopolitical and economic headwinds hold a recovery rally in check.

items.title: Stablecoins could weaken bank lending and monetary policy in Europe: ECB
items.summary: The European Central Bank warns in a new working paper that as stablecoin adoption grows, deposits may leave banks, affecting lending and monetary policy transmission.

items.title: Dollar surge pressures crypto markets after escalation in Iran conflict
items.summary: Risk assets fell across the board Tuesday as the U.S. dollar strengthened to a near two-month high following renewed military escalation in Iran, pressuring the crypto market.

items.title: ‘Scam token’ case against Uniswap dismissed by U.S. district judge in NYC
items.summary: District Judge says that due to the protocol’s decentralized nature, the identities of the scam token issuers are basically unknown, leaving plaintiffs with no identifiable defendant.

items.title: Bitcoin price chart ‘death cross’ is back, reviving late-cycle fears
items.summary: BTC price has slid about 35% on average over a month after similar trend line crossovers, keeping downside risk in focus for traders.

items.title: Kalshi, Polymarket face trading halt in Nevada after court rulings
items.summary: The two separate rulings add new regulatory pressure as prediction markets also face scrutiny over information advantages and suspected insider activity tied to event-driven contracts.

items.title: Japan prime minister Sanae Takaichi disavows Solana meme coin after it crashes by 75%
items.summary: Japan’s prime minister says she has no knowledge of or involvement in a Solana-based meme token that briefly reached a $27.7 million market cap before tumbling.

items.title: Riot reports record $647M revenue in 2025 as other Bitcoin miners struggle
items.summary: Riot Platforms posted record 2025 revenue of $647 million, driven by $576 million in Bitcoin mining revenue, while holding 18,005 BTC worth $1.6 billion.

items.title: Tether taps Deloitte for first USAT reserve report
items.summary: The report is a third-party attestation, which provides a snapshot of reserves at a specific point in time, rather than a full audit of the company's finances.

items.title: Bitcoin falls below $67,000 as U.S. equities slide and oil pushes higher
items.summary: Risk off sentiment builds ahead of Tuesday’s open, with investors moving into the dollar and watching energy markets amid ongoing Middle East tensions.

items.title: Spot Bitcoin ETFs see $458M in inflows as Mideast conflict widens
items.summary: US spot Bitcoin funds saw strong inflows and increasing volumes on Monday as institutional demand held up in spite of increasing Middle East tensions and broader market uncertainty.

items.summary: CORZ still holds under 1,000 BTC but look to "remain opportunistic" moving forward.

items.summary: Out of a fixed maximum supply of 21 million coins, more than 95% of all bitcoin that will ever exist is now in circulation.
